title: identity.v1
description: The identity contract: a scoped API token (subject + scopes + expiry, derived from the admin-api APIToken/`/internal/validate` shape) and an access decision (the canAccess verdict over a resource). Auth itself is transport-layer; these are the data shapes the identity core mints, validates, and decides over.

Scope.description: A capability a token carries. Mirrors admin-models VALID_SCOPES: bot (spawn/control bots), tx (read transcripts), browser (browser sessions).

ResourceKind.description: The kind of resource an access decision is made over. The three guarded read paths: a meeting's transcript, a recording, and the WS subscribe / agent read.

Action.description: The action attempted on a resource. Reads are what canAccess guards today; write/admin reserved for additive growth.

ScopedToken.description: A minted, scope-bounded credential. `subject` is the owning user id (admin-api User.id). The DB scopes column is authoritative upstream; here scopes[] is the sealed shape. `expires_at` null = non-expiring.

subject.description: Owning principal — the user id the token authenticates as.

scopes.description: Capabilities granted. Non-empty; each member is a Scope.

expires_at.description: RFC3339 instant after which the token is rejected. null = never expires.

email.description: Optional human identity carried for audit/attribution (admin-api User.email).

WorkspaceGrant.description: One workspace a dispatch may mount, at a mode (the write-access source of truth).

DispatchClaims.description: The verified claims of a per-dispatch SIGNED token — the chain of custody. Minted per dispatch by the Identity service (subject + launcher + workspace/tool grants), attested by the Runtime, verified at every boundary — never by the agent. The wire form is an opaque signed compact token; this is its decoded payload. Authority is re-derived from these claims, not from the (tamperable) request.

sub.description: subject — the principal acted on behalf of

lch.description: launcher — who/what triggered the dispatch (holds a delegation grant)

ws.description: workspace grants the workspace-store boundary enforces

tools.description: tool grants the MCP-gateway boundary enforces

AccessDecision.description: The verdict canAccess(subject, resource, action) returns. Default-deny: `allow=false` unless a policy explicitly grants. `reason` is a stable machine code, not free text.
